raw_results.query: best practices autonomous LLM agent memory management
new methods for LLM agent orchestration and multi-agent coordination

suggestions: Self-reflection and in-loop feedback**: Agents reflect at inference time using techniques like Reflexion (iterative prompt refinement), Self-Refine, RISE, STaR, SELF, and STaSC to learn self-correction; self-reward models score outputs for RL without separate reward models.[2]
Self-generated data and curricula**: Agents create their own training data, exemplars, or challenges (e.g., Self-Challenging for tool-use), filtering for quality to avoid poor signals.[2]

Self-adapting models**: Agents generate self-edit instructions, convert them to fine-tuning examples, and update weights via RL or supervised learning (e.g., SEAL); focuses on trustworthy improvement criteria.[2]
